steps.title: Identify the subject and its structure
steps.content: The blank comes after “Having proportionally large populations,” which is a gerund phrase acting as the subject of the sentence.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb agreement rule
steps.content:
Gerund phrases, regardless of their objects, function as singular subjects and require a singular verb. “Is” is the correct singular choice.

The other three are plural verbs (were, are, have been), which violate subject-verb agreement with this singular subject.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
